channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Network Performance Issues in IST
item.description: Apr 28, 09:21 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented, and we are monitoring the resultsApr 28, 08:45 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified, and we are implementing a fixApr 28, 08:41 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating network performance issues in IST

item.title: Spectrum Security Emerges From Stealth Mode With $19 Million
item.description: The threat detection startup will invest in accelerating its engineering and go-to-market efforts. The post Spectrum Security Emerges From Stealth Mode With $19 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Chinese Silk Typhoon Hacker Extradited to U.S. Over COVID Research Cyberattacks
item.description: A Chinese national accused of being a member of the Silk Typhoon hacking group has been extradited to the U.S. from Italy.  Xu Zewei, 34, was arrested in July 2025 by Italian authorities for his alleged links to the Chinese state-sponsored threat group and for orchestrating cyber attacks...

item.title: Microsoft Patches Entra ID Role Flaw That Enabled Service Principal Takeover
item.description: An administrative role meant for artificial intelligence (AI) agents within Microsoft Entra ID could enable privilege escalation and identity takeover attacks, according to new findings from Silverfort. Agent ID Administrator is a privileged built-in role introduced by Microsoft as part of its...

item.title: Medtronic Hack Confirmed After ShinyHunters Threatens Data Leak
item.description: The ShinyHunters cybercrime group claimed to have stolen 9 million records containing personal information from Medtronic. The post Medtronic Hack Confirmed After ShinyHunters Threatens Data Leak appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Microsoft Confirms Active Exploitation of Windows Shell CVE-2026-32202
item.description: Microsoft on Monday revised its advisory for a now-patched, high-severity security flaw impacting Windows Shell to acknowledge that it has been actively exploited in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-32202 (CVSS score: 4.3), a spoofing vulnerability that could allow an attacker to...

item.title: HONOR Integrates DeepSeek-V4 Model into YOYO Assistant
item.description: HONOR has integrated the DeepSeek-V4 AI model into its YOYO assistant, enhancing on-device capabilities such as reasoning, multi-turn dialogue, and multimodal interaction.

item.title: SF Aviation Completes First Rotor Flight Test of Hybrid eVTOL SF20V
item.description: SF Aviation completed the first rotor flight test of its hybrid eVTOL aircraft SF20V, designed for urban mobility with a range of up to 1,200 km.

item.title: Alibaba DAMO Academy Unveils AI Model for Non-Invasive Colorectal Cancer Screening
item.description: Alibaba DAMO Academy introduced an AI model capable of detecting colorectal cancer from routine CT scans without invasive preparation, achieving high accuracy in clinical validation.

item.title: [AINews] ImageGen is on the Path to AGI
item.description: reflecting on the continued GPT-Image-2 explosion

item.title: Lightelligence Lists in Hong Kong, Targeting Optical Computing for AI Infrastructure
item.description: Lightelligence debuted on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange with a market value exceeding $10 billion, focusing on silicon photonics solutions for AI computing infrastructure.

item.title: CP Group Backs AI Entertainment Startup Star Trail Interactive in Angel Round
item.description: StarTrail Interactive secured an angel round worth several million USD, backed by CP Group, marking the conglomerate’s first move into AI-driven entertainment and digital IP development.

item.title: Increased HTTP Errors in YUL
item.description: Apr 28, 05:00 UTCResolved - Cloudflare saw an increased level of HTTP errors in YUL between 04:55 UTC - 05:25 UTC.

item.title: CognitiveTwin: Robust Multi-Modal Digital Twins for Predicting Cognitive Decline in Alzheimer's Disease
item.description: arXiv:2604.22428v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Predicting individual cognitive decline in Alzheimer's disease (AD) is difficult due to the heterogeneity of disease progression. Reliable clinical tools require not only high accuracy but also fairness across demographics and robustness to missing...

item.title: AgentSearchBench: A Benchmark for AI Agent Search in the Wild
item.description: arXiv:2604.22436v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid growth of AI agent ecosystems is transforming how complex tasks are delegated and executed, creating a new challenge of identifying suitable agents for a given task. Unlike traditional tools, agent capabilities are often compositional and...

item.title: From Skills to Talent: Organising Heterogeneous Agents as a Real-World Company
item.description: arXiv:2604.22446v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Individual agent capabilities have advanced rapidly through modular skills and tool integrations, yet multi-agent systems remain constrained by fixed team structures, tightly coupled coordination logic, and session-bound learning. We argue that this...

item.title: Superminds Test: Actively Evaluating Collective Intelligence of Agent Society via Probing Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.22452v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Collective intelligence refers to the ability of a group to achieve outcomes beyond what any individual member can accomplish alone. As large language model agents scale to populations of millions, a key question arises: Does collective intelligence...

item.title: On the Hybrid Nature of ABPMS Process Frames and its Implications on Automated Process Discovery
item.description: arXiv:2604.22455v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A core component of any AI-Augmented Business Process Management System (ABPMS) is the process frame, which gives the system process-awareness and defines the boundaries in which the system must operate. Compared to traditional process models, the...

item.title: The Spectral Lifecycle of Transformer Training: Transient Compression Waves, Persistent Spectral Gradients, and the Q/K--V Asymmetry
item.description: arXiv:2604.22778v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present the first systematic study of weight matrix singular value spectra \emph{during} transformer pretraining, tracking full SVD decompositions of every weight matrix at 25-step intervals across three model scales (30M--285M parameters). We...

item.title: KARL: Mitigating Hallucinations in LLMs via Knowledge-Boundary-Aware Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.22779v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Enabling large language models (LLMs) to appropriately abstain from answering questions beyond their knowledge is crucial for mitigating hallucinations. While existing reinforcement learning methods foster autonomous abstention, they often compromise...

item.title: BiTA: Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit-Transformer Aggregator in a Temporal Graph Network Framework for Alert Prediction in Computer Networks
item.description: arXiv:2604.22781v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Proactive alert prediction in computer networks is critical for mitigating evolving cyber threats and enabling timely defensive actions. Temporal Graph Neural Networks (TGNs) provide a principled framework for modeling time-evolving interactions;...

item.title: Stochastic KV Routing: Enabling Adaptive Depth-Wise Cache Sharing
item.description: arXiv:2604.22782v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Serving transformer language models with high throughput requires caching Key-Values (KVs) to avoid redundant computation during autoregressive generation. The memory footprint of KV caching is significant and heavily impacts serving costs. This work...

item.title: Parameter Efficiency Is Not Memory Efficiency: Rethinking Fine-Tuning for On-Device LLM Adaptation
item.description: arXiv:2604.22783v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) has become the standard for adapting large language models (LLMs). In this work we challenge the wide-spread assumption that parameter efficiency equates memory efficiency and on-device adaptability. We show that...

item.title: Dissociating Decodability and Causal Use in Bracket-Sequence Transformers
item.description: arXiv:2604.22128v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When trained on tasks requiring an understanding of hierarchical structure, transformers have been found to represent this hierarchy in distinct ways: in the geometry of the residual stream, and in stack-like attention patterns maintaining a last-in,...

item.title: SHAPE: Unifying Safety, Helpfulness and Pedagogy for Educational LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2604.22134v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have been widely explored in educational scenarios. We identify a critical vulnerability in current educational LLMs, pedagogical jailbreaks, where students use answer-inducing prompts to elicit solutions rather than...

item.title: Voice Under Revision: Large Language Models and the Normalization of Personal Narrative
item.description: arXiv:2604.22142v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study examines how large language model rewriting alters the style and narrative texture of personal narratives. It analyzes 300 personal narratives rewritten by three frontier LLMs under three prompt conditions: generic improvement,...

item.title: When AI Speaks, Whose Values Does It Express? A Cross-Cultural Audit of Individualism-Collectivism Bias in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.22153v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When you ask an AI assistant for advice about your career, your marriage, or a conflict with your family, does it give you the same answer regardless of where you are from? We tested this systematically by presenting three leading AI systems (Claude...

item.title: Fine-Grained Analysis of Shared Syntactic Mechanisms in Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.22166v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While language models demonstrate sophisticated syntactic capabilities, the extent to which their internal mechanisms align with cross-constructional principles studied in linguistics remains poorly understood. This study investigates whether models...

item.title: Adaptive Ultrasound Imaging with Physics-Informed NV-Raw2Insights-US AI
Physical AI that Moves the World — Qasar Younis & Peter Ludwig, Applied Intuition
item.description: Applied Intuition puts the AI in mining rigs, drones, trucks, warships and physical vehicles in the most adversarial environments imaginable. We dive in with their CEO and CTO as they emerge.

item.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are going to court over OpenAI’s future
item.description: After a yearslong legal feud, Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman are heading to trial this week in Northern California in a case that could have sweeping consequences. Ahead of OpenAI’s highly anticipated IPO, the court could rule on whether the company is allowed to exist as a for-profit...

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Apr 27, 17:01 UTCUpdate - Copilot Cloud Agent (CCA) jobs using the Codex agent are failing after starting. To avoid this issue, please choose a different agent. We are investigating the cause and working towards remediationApr 27, 16:48 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of impacted...

item.title: Diffusion-Guided Feature Selection via Nishimori Temperature: Noise-Based Spectral Embedding
item.description: We propose Noise-Based Spectral Embedding (NBSE), a physics-informed framework for selecting informative features from high-dimensional data without greedy search. NBSE constructs a sparse similarity graph on the samples and identifies the Nishimori temperature $β_N$ the critical inverse...

item.title: GitHub search is degraded
item.description: Apr 27, 16:53 UTCUpdate - Pull Requests is experiencing degraded performance. We are continuing to investigate.Apr 27, 16:39 UTCUpdate - Packages is experiencing degraded performance. We are continuing to investigate.Apr 27, 16:36 UTCUpdate - Issues is experiencing degraded performance. We are...

item.title: DepthKV: Layer-Dependent KV Cache Pruning for Long-Context LLM Inference
item.description: Long-context reasoning is a critical capability of large language models (LLMs), enabling applications such as long-document understanding, summarization, and code generation. However, efficient autoregressive inference relies on the key-value (KV) cache, whose memory footprint grows linearly with...

item.title: The missing step between hype and profit
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. In February, I picked up a flyer at an anti-AI march in London. I can’t say for sure whether or not its writers meant to riff on South Park’s underpants gnomes....

item.title: Elevated billing related errors on Claude.ai
item.description: Apr 27, 15:18 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 27, 14:11 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Checkmarx Confirms GitHub Repository Data Posted on Dark Web After March 23 Attack
item.description: Checkmarx has disclosed that its ongoing investigation tied to the supply chain security incident has revealed that a cybercriminal group published data related to the company on the dark web. "Based on current evidence, we believe this data originated from Checkmarx's GitHub repository, and that...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Fast16 Malware, XChat Launch, Federal Backdoor, AI Employee Tracking & More
item.description: Everything is dumb again. This week feels broken in a very familiar way. Old tricks are back. New tools are doing shady crap. Supply chains got hit. Fake help desks worked. Weird research showed how easy some attacks still are. Most of it feels like stuff we should have fixed years ago. Bad...

item.title: Incomplete Windows Patch Opens Door to Zero-Click Attacks
item.description: The initial vulnerability was exploited by Russia-linked APT28 in attacks against Ukraine and EU countries. The post Incomplete Windows Patch Opens Door to Zero-Click Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Rebuilding the data stack for AI
item.description: Artificial intelligence may be dominating boardroom agendas, but many enterprises are discovering that the biggest obstacle to meaningful adoption is the state of their data. While consumer-facing AI tools have dazzled users with speed and ease, enterprise leaders are discovering that deploying AI...

item.title: OpenSSH Flaw Allowing Full Root Shell Access Lurked for 15 Years
item.description: A code reuse issue enabled comma characters in certificate principals to be interpreted as list separators. The post OpenSSH Flaw Allowing Full Root Shell Access Lurked for 15 Years appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: DeepSeek’s latest AI breakthrough, and the race to build world models
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Three reasons why DeepSeek’s new model matters On Friday, Chinese AI firm DeepSeek released a preview of V4, its long-awaited new flagship model....

item.title: Malicious AI Prompt Injection Attacks Increasing, but Sophistication Still Low: Google
item.description: The tech giant found that many indirect prompt injection attempts are harmless, but some malicious exploits have also been identified. The post Malicious AI Prompt Injection Attacks Increasing, but Sophistication Still Low: Google appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Could your choice of metrics be harming your SOC?
item.description: Poor metrics can render a well-intentioned security operation centre entirely ineffective.

item.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
item.description: Google DeepMind and Korea partner to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using frontier AI models

item.title: Turnstile Challenge Issues
item.description: Apr 27, 06:15 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating a potential issue with the Turnstile challenge platform. Users may experience failed challenges solve attempts.Further details will be provided as more information becomes available.

item.title: AgenticCache: Cache-Driven Asynchronous Planning for Embodied AI Agents
item.description: Embodied AI agents increasingly rely on large language models (LLMs) for planning, yet per-step LLM calls impose severe latency and cost. In this paper, we show that embodied tasks exhibit strong plan locality, where the next plan is largely predictable from the current one. Building on this, we...

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Brazil
item.description: Apr 27, 04:50 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 27, 04:25 UTCMonitoring - Cloudflare is investigating issues with Network Performance in Brazil. Customers may observe increase disconnects and CNI tunnel degradation. We are working to analyze and mitigate this problem. More updates...

item.title: Poster: ClawdGo: Endogenous Security Awareness Training for Autonomous AI Agents
item.description: Autonomous AI agents deployed on platforms such as OpenClaw face prompt injection, memory poisoning, supply-chain attacks, and social engineering, yet existing defences address only the platform perimeter, leaving the agent's own threat judgement entirely untrained. We present ClawdGo, a framework...
